action.reasoning: Observation: The learned ensemble weights (favoring cleaned models heavily) produced the exact same 76.73% test accuracy as the uniform ensemble, indicating the weight tuning overfitted to the cleaned training labels and does not generalize. Analysis: With only ~2.2 minutes remaining, no training or lengthy local inference is possible. A quick, theoretically-grounded change is to switch from logit averaging to probability (softmax) averaging across models; this reduces sensitivity to models with different logit scales/calibrations and can improve ensemble robustness. I will also revert to uniform weights to avoid overfitted weights. Decision: 1) Overwrite ensemble_weights.json with uniform weights. 2) Rewrite predict.py to average softmax probabilities instead of logits. 3) Submit evaluation immediately with no local command to avoid timeout.
